tldr.bestFor: Teams turning PPTs, PDFs, blogs, or scripts into narrated baseline videos with manual cleanup and language control
tldr.notFor: Creators expecting a nearly finished PPT-to-video conversion, workspace-wide pronunciation rules, or effortless no-code automation
tldr.why: Official video assets show useful baseline generation, pronunciation mapping, phrase-level B-roll, and automation hooks, but also clear cleanup and setup friction

miniTest.prompt: Import a 10-15 slide training deck or PDF and turn it into a 60-90 second narrated video. Track the cleanup needed for script, B-roll timing, avatar placement, pronunciation, and translated output.

checklist.label: Baseline generation
checklist.value: Does PPT/PDF import create useful scene containers, script, and voiceover?

checklist.label: Manual cleanup
checklist.value: Count B-roll inserts, avatar repositioning, audio rebalance, and slide replacements

checklist.label: Pronunciation map
checklist.value: Test whether a brand term applies across scenes and only changes spoken output

checklist.label: B-roll precision
checklist.value: Compare phrase-level B-roll insertion against autopick scene-level media

checklist.label: Translation rerender
checklist.value: Check whether translated voice/text changes break avatar lip-sync until regenerated

checklist.label: Automation setup
checklist.value: Record API key, Template ID, Voice ID, Folder ID, webhook, and filter friction

checklist.label: Apply-all risk
checklist.value: Check how global voice/layout changes affect rerender scope and cleanup cost

miniTest.verdict: Use this as a source-backed workflow check, not a completed hands-on run. Fliki looks strongest as a baseline-generation and voice-control tool, but PPT/PDF finishing, automation setup, and translation rerenders need hands-on validation.

useCases.title: PPT/PDF training drafts
useCases.why: Official material supports slide-to-scene baseline generation with auto script and voiceover, but the valuable framing is first draft plus cleanup rather than publish-ready conversion

useCases.title: Pronunciation-controlled voiceovers
useCases.why: Pronunciation map is a high-value spoken-output control for brand terms and names, while on-screen text and subtitles still need separate review

useCases.title: Automation-assisted publishing
useCases.why: API, template, voice, folder, webhook, and filter dependencies make Fliki useful for automated pipelines, but not frictionless

keyFeatures: PPT/PDF import creates baseline scene containers with auto script and voiceover
Pronunciation map acts like a project-level spoken-output rule, not a workspace-wide policy
Phrase-level B-roll can be tied to highlighted script text; autopick remains scene-level and less precise
Translation changes voice and text, but media or slide content may still need manual localization
Voice/text changes can require manual avatar regeneration to restore lip-sync
Automation depends on API key, Template ID, Voice ID, Folder ID, webhook, and filters
Free plan exports include visible watermark
Free exports are 720p; paid plans support 1080p
Free includes 5 minutes monthly; paid offers higher allowances
Credits deducted based on total video length
Exports: MP4, MOV, MP3, WAV supported
Subtitle downloads in SRT and VTT formats
Stock library includes millions of royalty-free media assets
Web-based; Chrome is the recommended browser
Cancel anytime via account billing dashboard

pros: Strong official evidence for PPT/PDF baseline generation
Pronunciation map gives useful spoken-output control
Phrase-level B-roll provides more precise timing than scene-level autopick
Automation hooks are concrete enough for pipeline experiments
Straightforward export format options, including audio-only
Separate subtitle downloads in SRT and VTT
cons: PPT/PDF output still needs manual cleanup before publishing
Automation setup is configuration-heavy, not no-code effortless
Translation can create avatar lip-sync rerender work
Autopick media is not proven to be phrase-accurate
Free plan watermark limits professional publishing
Commercial use requires a paid plan
Refunds are case-by-case at sole discretion

verdict.bottomLine: Fliki is best for creators who need fast blog-to-video repurposing and voiceover-first workflows. The main trade-off is visual accuracy: auto-selected stock footage often misses context and requires manual replacement. Free plan exports are watermarked at 720p; commercial rights and 1080p exports require a paid plan.

faqs.question: Can I use Fliki for free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Fliki offers a Free plan to try the product. Free plan exports include a visible watermark.

faqs.question: Does the Free plan include a watermark?
faqs.answer: Yes—videos generated on the Free plan contain a Fliki watermark. If you need clean exports, you'll likely need a paid plan.

faqs.question: What export resolution do I get on Free vs paid plans?
faqs.answer: Free users export HD 720p videos. Paid plans support Full HD 1080p exports.

faqs.question: How do minutes/credits work?
faqs.answer: Credits are deducted based on the total length of the video. Free includes 5 minutes monthly, while paid plans provide more minutes.

faqs.question: Do paid plans include commercial rights?
faqs.answer: Yes—commercial rights are listed on paid subscription plans. Free plan commercial rights are not granted.

faqs.question: What formats can I export?
faqs.answer: Fliki supports exporting in MP4, MOV, MP3, and WAV formats. This lets you export video or audio-only versions.

faqs.question: Can I download subtitles separately?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can download subtitles in both SRT and VTT formats. This is useful for publishing captions outside the editor.

faqs.question: What browser should I use?
faqs.answer: It's a web-based app and Chrome is the recommended browser. Other browsers may work, but performance can vary.

faqs.question: How do I cancel my subscription?
faqs.answer: You can cancel anytime by going to Account and selecting Manage billing. This is handled from your billing dashboard.

faqs.question: What's the refund policy?
faqs.answer: Refund requests may be considered case-by-case and granted at the company's sole discretion. Don't assume refunds are guaranteed if you forget to cancel.

faqs.question: Will the AI automatically pick relevant stock footage?
faqs.answer: User reports: auto-selected stock visuals can miss the script's intended context. Plan time to replace clips manually for best results.

faqs.question: What are the minimum hardware/RAM requirements?
faqs.answer: No specific hardware/RAM thresholds are confirmed. If you see lag, try Chrome and a faster connection first.

Official YouTube channel and product dem.howToVerify: Review Fliki's official YouTube channel and official product demo videos.

Official product pages and documentation.howToVerify: Check Fliki's official website, product pages, and documentation.
